question: Spread: West Bromwich Albion FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game, scheduled for April 6 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "West Bromwich Albion FC" if West Bromwich Albion FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Blackburn Rovers FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Blackburn Rovers FC vs. West Bromwich Albion FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the EFL Championship game, scheduled for April 6 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans toward West Bromwich Albion in this EFL Championship matchup at Ewood Park, driven by Blackburn Rovers' ongoing struggles under interim management following John Eustace's sacking after a six-game winless run and porous defense conceding 13 goals lately. West Brom, firmly in playoff contention, enter unbeaten in seven across all competitions with strong away form (W3 D1 L1 recently) and key attackers like Jed Wallace and Grady Diangana available per official reports. Blackburn's home record sits at just two wins from nine, while head-to-heads show West Brom unbeaten in the last three meetings. Fatigue from midweek fixtures could factor, but Baggies' momentum shapes implied probabilities.

events.description: More markets for the EFL Championship game, scheduled for April 6 at 10:00 AM ET.

events.description: More markets for the EFL Championship game, scheduled for April 6 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment heavily favors Southampton in this EFL Cup clash, with market odds reflecting their superior Championship pedigree and attacking firepower against Wrexham's scrappy League One defense. eventMetadata.context_description: Millwall's formidable home record at The Den drives trader consensus toward the Lions in this Championship clash, with the hosts unbeaten in their last six outings there and conceding just three goals. Norwich City struggles away, winless in five road games this season amid defensive lapses, tilting implied probabilities further. Recent developments include Millwall regaining midfielder George Saville from suspension for midfield control, while Norwich misses forward Josh Sargent to a hamstring strain per official reports, weakening their attack. Both sides enter on winning streaks, but Millwall's rest advantage after midweek rest and historical edge (3-1 last meeting) underscore shifting market dynamics ahead of kickoff.

question: Spread: Norwich City FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game, scheduled for April 6 at 8:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Norwich City FC" if Norwich City FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Millwall FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Portsmouth FC vs. Oxford United FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the EFL Championship game, scheduled for April 6 at 7:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Portsmouth FC holds a slight edge in trader consensus for their Championship clash with Oxford United FC, driven by Pompey's strong home form at Fratton Park where they've won four of six league games this season, conceding just three goals. Oxford, newly promoted like Portsmouth, sit near the relegation zone after a dismal run of one win in nine, hampered by injuries to key midfielder Cameron Brannagan (doubtful, per official report) and forward Mark Harris. Recent head-to-heads favor Portsmouth (3-1 aggregate last season), but Oxford's rest advantage after midweek rest could spark an upset. Momentum tilts homeward amid tightening playoff race implications.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fortuna Düsseldorf's solid home record at Merkur Spiel-Arena drives trader consensus toward the hosts in their 2. Bundesliga clash with Hertha BSC, where implied probabilities reflect Düsseldorf's edge from recent form (three wins in five) and Hertha's road struggles (winless in last four away). Hertha's attacking firepower, led by Ibrahim Maza's six goals, keeps markets tight, but Düsseldorf's defensive solidity under coach Frank Kramer limits concessions. No major injuries reported officially, though watch for late lineups; Düsseldorf's playoff push adds motivation versus Hertha's mid-table chase, with weather and referee tendencies as minor wildcards.

question: Spread: TSV Fortuna 95 Düsseldorf (-2.5)
description:
In the upcoming 2. Bundesliga game, scheduled for March 22 at 8:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "TSV Fortuna 95 Düsseldorf" if TSV Fortuna 95 Düsseldorf win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Hertha BSC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Military action against Iran ends on March 18, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next date Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30), on which neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate. 

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. If strikes could be verified under the prior rule for each listed day, this market will resolve to "Military action continues through March 31."

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors military action against Iran persisting through March 31 (90.5%), reflecting persistent regional tensions from Israel's October 26 strikes on Iranian military sites, which caused limited damage but drew vows of retaliation from Tehran. Ongoing proxy conflicts involving Hezbollah in Lebanon and Houthi disruptions in the Red Sea reinforce expectations of sustained pressure, with no imminent de-escalation signals from official statements. Iran's nuclear advancements and U.S. election uncertainties further bolster this view, as crowdsourced odds highlight low base rates for rapid resolutions in such standoffs. Realistic challenges include a surprise diplomatic breakthrough or verified ceasefire in related fronts, potentially shifting probabilities ahead of key talks.

If strikes could be verified under the prior rule for each listed day, this market will resolve to "Military action continues through March 31."

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the "Military action against Iran ends by" market leans toward quick de-escalation following Israel's limited October 26 airstrikes on Iranian missile production sites, which avoided nuclear facilities, oil infrastructure, or high civilian casualties. Tehran downplayed the strikes as ineffective and has not launched direct retaliation, amid internal economic pressures and signals of restraint from Supreme Leader Khamenei. U.S. officials urged proportionality, while diplomatic channels remain open. Odds reflect low escalation risk, with traders watching for proxy responses via Hezbollah or Houthis rather than full war; key upcoming events include potential UN Security Council sessions and Iran's 60-day response window under its narrative.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate on a full calendar day by the listed date, Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. 

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the "Military action against Iran ends by" market leans toward quick de-escalation following Israel's limited October 26 airstrikes on Iranian missile production sites, which avoided nuclear facilities, oil infrastructure, or high civilian casualties. Tehran downplayed the strikes as ineffective and has not launched direct retaliation, amid internal economic pressures and signals of restraint from Supreme Leader Khamenei. U.S. officials urged proportionality, while diplomatic channels remain open. Odds reflect low escalation risk, with traders watching for proxy responses via Hezbollah or Houthis rather than full war; key upcoming events include potential UN Security Council sessions and Iran's 60-day response window under its narrative.

question: Lightning vs. Sabres
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 6 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Lightning win, the market will resolve to "Lightning".
If the Sabres win, the market will resolve to "Sabres".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tampa Bay Lightning hold a trader-favored edge over the Buffalo Sabres heading into their matchup, driven by superior goaltending from Andrei Vasilevskiy, who's posted a .925 save percentage in recent starts following his early-season recovery. The Bolts boast a 6-2-1 record with strong road splits, while Buffalo's 5-3-2 mark tempers enthusiasm despite a hot 4-1-1 homestand fueled by Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen's emergence. Key developments include Tampa's full health on defense after Mikhail Sergachev's return and Buffalo's power-play woes at 12.5% efficiency. Traders note the Lightning's 7-3 edge in recent head-to-heads, though Sabres' rest advantage and rivalry intensity could spark an upset amid Atlantic Division tightness.

question: Lightning vs. Sabres: O/U 4.5
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 6 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Lightning and Sabres combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Both rebuilding franchises enter their matchup with 0-3 records, fueling tight trader consensus around a low-scoring affair as the Blackhawks host the Sharks. Chicago leans on Connor Bedard's offensive spark amid defensive woes without Seth Jones (foot, out indefinitely), while San Jose counters with rookie Macklin Celebrini's early promise but lacks depth after trading key pieces. Recent blowout losses highlight leaky goaltending on both sides—Petr Mrazek (3.74 GAA) and Vitek Vanecek (4.41 GAA)—with historical trends showing Sharks' poor road play (1-8-1 last 10 away). Momentum favors no outright favorite, as rest advantages and home-ice edge keep implied probabilities hovering near even.

eventMetadata.context_description: Los Angeles Kings hold a trader consensus edge over the Nashville Predators heading into Friday's matchup at Crypto.com Arena, driven by home-ice advantage and Cam Talbot's elite .941 save percentage across six starts, fueling a 3-2-1 record. Nashville's 4-1-0 hot streak, led by Filip Forsberg's six goals, tempers the odds, but Juuse Saros faces scrutiny post-injury while the Predators' road defense has leaked 3.2 goals per game. Recent Kings power-play dominance (28.6%) exploits Nashville's middling penalty kill, though Pacific-Central division implications heighten upset potential amid both teams' early momentum.

Shenzhen, 15th in the standings, snapped a four-match skid with a 1-1 draw at Qingdao West Coast but concedes 1.8 goals per game at home; key midfielder Yue Peng is doubtful with a hamstring strain per official reports. Wuhan, 10th, rides momentum from back-to-back wins including 2-1 over Meizhou Hakka, boasting a solid away record (unbeaten in four), but top scorer Pedro Henrique's suspension looms large. Head-to-head favors Shenzhen (2-1 aggregate), yet fatigue from midweek cups tilts odds toward under 2.5 goals at 60% implied probability.

question: T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka: Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Afghanistan and Sri Lanka scheduled for March 17 2026 in T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Afghanistan boasts a perfect 4-0 record in bilateral T20Is at their UAE neutral venue, where conditions suit their mystery spinners against Sri Lanka's vulnerable middle order. Sri Lanka counters with pace from Nuwan Thushara and Matheesha Pathirana, but their batting faltered in the recent T20 World Cup, losing momentum post-group stage exit. Watch for toss impact on dew-affected chases and Afghanistan's opener Ibrahim Zadran's form, as schedule rest favors the hosts ahead of back-to-back games.

question: T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka: Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka - Who wins the toss?
description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Afghanistan and Sri Lanka scheduled for 2026-03-17 in T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

question: T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka: Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka - Most Sixes Afghanistan Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Afghanistan and Sri Lanka scheduled for 2026-03-17 in T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Afghanistan will be considered correct if Afghanistan is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Sri Lanka.The outcome corresponding to Sri Lanka will be considered correct if Sri Lanka is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Afghanistan.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Will Tyler1 shave his head by June 1, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tyler1 shaves his head by June 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purpose of this market, “shaving his head" refers to a clearly visible and noticeable change to a fully shaved/buzzed head. 

Small adjustments, minor trims, buzzing just the sides, or natural minor thinning that doesn't dramatically alter his appearance will not qualify. 

Taking a little snip, minor trim, or casual buzz on stream (or in a video) also does not count. It must be a real, substantial shave that noticeably changes his appearance from the hair style he's maintained.

The resolution source for this market will be photographic or video evidence.

question: SAVE Act becomes law by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any bill, measure, or resolution that requires proof of U.S. citizenship as a condition for registering to vote or to vote in U.S. federal elections is passed by both chambers of the U.S. Congress and signed into law, or is otherwise enacted into U.S. federal law, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Examples of qualifying legislation include H.R. 22, the “SAVE Act,” and H.R. 7296, the “SAVE America Act.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: SAVE Act becomes law by...?

eventMetadata.context_description: House passage of the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act in July 2024 by a 220-196 vote marks the primary advancement, mandating proof of citizenship for federal voter registration, yet Senate Democratic leadership has blocked consideration amid claims of unnecessary barriers. Trader consensus implies low near-term odds for enactment by year-end, reflecting partisan divide and slim Republican House majority vulnerable to losses. Pivotal November 5 elections loom: a Republican Senate flip and Trump victory could revive the bill in the 119th Congress starting January 2025, while Democratic retention sustains veto risk. Lame-duck session maneuvers remain possible but unlikely without bipartisan buy-in.

question: Will Clavicular cry on stream by May 1, 2026
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Clavicular visibly sheds tears that can be clearly observed on their face during any live stream broadcast by May 1, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The 'crying' must occur while Clavicular is live on stream and the tears must be visible on camera. Crying that occurs off-camera, is only mentioned or described verbally, or occurs outside of a live stream (e.g. in real life off-stream or in edited content) will not count toward this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source is Clavicular's Kick.com channel (https://www.kick.com/clavicular) or credible video clips from the stream; however, a consensus of credible reporting or widely circulated video evidence may also be used.

question: Will Clavicular go clubbing 0-2 times in March?
description:
This market will resolve to the number of times Clavicular goes clubbing during the month of March 2026.

"Times" refers to discrete instances (separate nights) where Clavicular is physically present inside a nightclub, club, or similar nightlife venue (with music, dancing, and/or bottle service) for any duration of time. 

Club appearances prior to this market being put up do not count.

Streaming from inside the club, posting videos or photos from the club, or being visibly filmed while clubbing will all count as evidence. Simply walking past a club, going to a bar that is not a nightclub, attending private parties, or off-stream club visits with no public evidence will not qualify. 

Each separate night he enters a club counts as one instance, even if he visits multiple clubs in the same night.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Kick.com (e.g., https://www.kick.com/clavicular), credible video clips, photos, or stories posted by Clavicular himself, or a consensus of credible reporting or widely circulated video evidence showing him inside a club during March 2026.

question: Cuban regime falls in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Communist Party of Cuba (PCC) ceases to exercise de facto governing control over Cuba by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A “Yes” resolution requires a clear and widely reported break from the PCC’s historical control over the government of Cuba. This may include events such as the overthrow or dissolution of the PCC and its replacement by a new government or transitional authority, the constitutional removal of the PCC’s status as the sole ruling party followed by a transfer of governing power to a different political entity, or the holding of multi-party national elections that result in a government not controlled by the PCC. A “Yes” resolution does not require the formal dissolution of the PCC, provided the PCC no longer exercises de facto governing control over Cuba.

Leadership changes within the PCC, including replacement of the First Secretary, or governmental reforms that preserve the PCC’s de facto governing control over Cuba, will not suffice. Partial loss of territory, civil unrest, or challenges by rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the PCC no longer administers the majority of the Cuban population within Cuba.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low odds of the Cuban regime collapsing in 2026, driven primarily by Havana's firm control over security forces amid persistent economic turmoil and energy blackouts. Recent nationwide power outages in October 2024 sparked localized protests in cities like Santiago, but authorities swiftly arrested demonstrators, preventing escalation, as reported in credible outlets. The government's one-party system endures despite GDP contraction, hyperinflation, and mass emigration, bolstered by military loyalty and aid from allies like Russia and Venezuela. No credible signs of internal fractures or organized opposition have emerged, aligning with historical resilience during crises like the 1990s Special Period, though US sanctions remain a pressure point.

question: Neutrl FDV above $20M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Neutrl's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Neutrl (https://x.com/Neutrl) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will MicroStrategy announce holding 1M+ BTC by December 31, 2026?-bV81
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if MicroStrategy Incorporated or Michael Saylor officially announce that the company’s total Bitcoin holdings are equal to or greater than the number specified in the title at any point by 11:59 PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official announcements from MicroStrategy or Michael Saylor as to their total BTC holdings.

For reference, MicroStrategy’s reported BTC holdings can be tracked at: https://www.strategy.com/purchases
